translation.en: I didn't go because it was raining.

notes_md:
**vì thế** = *therefore, as a result, so*. It shows a result that comes from what was just said.

It links two clauses: first the reason, then the result with **vì thế**.  

It sounds a bit more formal and written than **nên** (which is more common in speech). Similar to **do đó** (more formal). Use it to clearly show cause → result.
notes:
vì thế = therefore, as a result, so. It shows a result that comes from what was just said.

It links two clauses: first the reason, then the result with vì thế.  

It sounds a bit more formal and written than nên (which is more common in speech). Similar to do đó (more formal). Use it to clearly show cause → result.
exampleSentences: Trời đang mưa rất to, vì thế anh không ra ngoài tối nay.
Em đã học rất chăm chỉ, vì thế em đã đạt điểm cao trong kỳ thi.
Chị ấy bị ốm, vì thế chị ấy không đến buổi họp hôm nay.

translation.en: I didn't go because it was raining.

exampleSentencesTranslated: It is raining heavily, therefore I am not going out tonight.
I studied very hard, therefore I got a high score in the exam.
She is sick, therefore she did not come to the meeting today.

definitions.en: A phrase used to indicate the result or reason of a previously mentioned situation.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ kết quả hoặc lý do của một sự việc đã nói trước đó.

example.en: It was raining heavily, so we had to stay at home.
example.vi: Trời mưa rất to, vì thế chúng tôi phải ở nhà.
